asset_name: Shadow Safe Zone Overlay Tile (96x96 static)
shared_properties: The overlay uses a 96x96 px modular tile that can repeat across floor regions where Umbra is protected from exposure buildup.
Core palette metadata remains primary #C9A84C, background #0D0B14, and accent #D4682A while teal shadow tones stay inside specs.colors_hex.
The tile keeps soft interior falloff and sharp outer-edge readability so players can judge safety boundaries quickly.
differences: The center area is deepest teal with faint gold dust specks that mark a stable refuge from direct light.
The outer 8 px rim brightens slightly to communicate the border where safe shadow ends and beam danger begins.
Subtle floor texture shows through the overlay so it reads as lighting state over geometry rather than a new floor tile.

reference_images.label: gdd_markdown
reference_images.purpose: Grounds exposure reduction in full shadow and the distinction between safe and dangerous light coverage.

reference_images.label: moodboard
reference_images.purpose: Grounds the teal refuge treatment used for shadow-safe regions.

reference_images.label: umbra_character
reference_images.purpose: Grounds Umbra silhouette contrast against protected darkness.

bevy_constraints.bundle_strategy: SpriteBundle layered under characters and over static floor geometry.
bevy_constraints.asset_loading: Load the shadow-safe tile during FloorLoading and stamp it through level data where safe darkness exists.
runtime_notes: Multiply this overlay with beam masks and floor textures rather than replacing the base geometry art.
Keep alpha edges authored in-texture so the boundary remains deterministic.
Render the overlay below Umbra and Echo silhouettes.

context.usage: Authorable world overlay that marks player-safe darkness zones and visually contrasts with exposure hazards.

overlay_notes: Bright boundary rim thickness is 8 px.
Center gradient preserves at least 60 percent dark coverage.
Dust specks occupy fewer than 20 visible pixels.

acceptance_criteria: Overlay tile is exactly 96x96 px and keeps a readable safe-dark center with a controlled boundary rim.
Teal shadow treatment remains distinct from beam hazard whites and amber emitter accents.
Underlying floor silhouette can still be perceived through the overlay so the region reads as lighting state rather than solid geometry.
